days.label: Monday
days.text: Monday itself eases you into the week. Runners can lace up for Skirack's Run Club at 5:45, a casual paced community run out of the Main Street shop that shoots for 3 to 5 miles with groups by pace. Riders get the same energy in Williston, where Catamount hosts a Novice Monday group mountain bike ride from 6 to 8. If your speed is quieter, the Monday Night Open Mic at Pearl Street Pub in Essex Junction gets going at 6, and parents can start the day early with the free Family Concert Series at Dorothy Alling Memorial Library in Williston at 11:15, where Green Mountain Chamber Music Festival students play a short set with a sweet treat after. And from our corner, our group's Sunset Paddle at Shelburne Bay launches at 7 tonight. Bring a kayak or board, catch the sunset from the water, and come say hi.

days.label: Tuesday
days.text: Tuesday is the big ticket day. Gillian Welch and David Rawlings ($56) play the Ben & Jerry's Concerts on the Green at Shelburne Museum at 7, with kids 12 and under free on the lawn. Earlier in the afternoon, the Old North End celebrates the Pomeroy Park playground grand opening from 2 to 3 with a ribbon cutting alongside the mayor. Curious minds can drop into Where is AI in 2026 and Where is it Going? at Fletcher Free Library at 5:30, a free discussion workshop run through the AI Safety Awareness Meetup where the talking is the point. Night owls can close it out on the waterfront with Swiss psych funk outfit L'Eclair with Two Sev and DJ Rice Pilaf at Foam Brewers at 8:30, or two step into Honky Tonk Tuesday ($10) at Radio Bean at 8:30.

days.label: Wednesday
days.text: Wednesday is stacked even with the clouds hanging around. The monster show is Trombone Shorty with JJ Grey & Mofro on the Green at Shelburne Museum at 7, though fair warning, Higher Ground lists it as sold out, so it is waitlist or ticket exchange territory now. Colchester counters with the Sam Mazza's Summer Concert Series featuring B-Town from 6 to 8 at the farm market on Lavigne Road. Freelancers and remote workers can talk shop at After Hours at Foam Brewers , a casual 21+ networking evening at 6. The Craftsbury Chamber Players bring their summer season to the Elley-Long Music Center in Colchester at 7:30 with students $10 and kids free, and GYMSHORTS with POLKAROBICS, the Eyetraps, and the Skizm turns Radio Bean loud at 8. Our own Pick-up Basketball at Pomeroy Park runs 5:30 to 7:30, so come out and run some full court games.

days.label: Thursday
days.text: Thursday might be the best all around day of the week, sunny, 79, and absolutely stacked. The bricks host Party On The Bricks from 5:30 to 8, free on Church Street with the Fly-By Ride and music on the Opera Block. South Burlington answers with SB Nite Out at 5:30, free live music from A House on Fire plus a blanket giveaway for the first 500 through the gate. Williston gets GARTH Live , a high octane Garth Brooks tribute, at the Maple Tree Place Summer Concert Series at 6, and Shelburne Museum keeps its own streak going with the free Shelburne Summer Nights from 5 to 7:30. Out at Isham Family Farm, loon biologist Eric Hanson gives a free Loon Talk from 5:30 to 7:30, timely since the lake's loon pair hatched two chicks this summer. Animal people can paint their own companion at Paint Your Pet at Queen City Cat Lounge from 6 to 8 (register by Wednesday), trivia types get Music Bingo at Sparky's in Essex Junction at 6:30, and film buffs can catch Kurosawa's The Hidden Fortress with the Vermont International Film Festival at 7. Comedy fans should grab Molly Kearney ($25) at Vermont Comedy Club at 7, the SNL alum in for one night. Later on, Winooski's Monkey House hosts King Garbage with C.Shreve the Professor and 3rd Chamber ($10) , doors at 8. And if you feel like a short drive, the free Sticks and Stones concert at Snow Farm Vineyard in South Hero runs 6:30 to 8:30, lawn chairs and sunset views encouraged.
